From: Michael Thompson <m.thompson@rmrenewables.com>
To: Sarah Klein <sarah.klein@clearcreektitle.com>
Date: 2025-10-07 09:42 MST
Subject: Re: Clarification on Evergreen Ridge lot configuration

Hi Sarah,

Thanks again for taking the time to go through the survey and old subdivision documents with me on the phone yesterday. I know this Evergreen Ridge situation is a little messy, and I really appreciate your patience in helping us make sure the refinance doesn’t get tripped up.

Per your request, here is the owner contact information you can put on file for anything related to 742 Evergreen Ridge Road:

Michael James Thompson
742 Evergreen Ridge Road
Idaho Springs, CO 80452
Home phone: (303) 555-2184
Cell: 720-441-9936
Email: this address is best during the day.

To restate what we discussed: the main reason I’m pushing to get the lot status cleaned up now is that we’re in the middle of a refinance, and the underwriter flagged the way the house straddles the two historic parcels. I also want to make sure that if I ever decide to sell off one of these pieces, I don’t run into problems with a title company discovering that the split was never actually legal under county code.

Timing has also become important because my employer, Rocky Mountain Renewables, put me on a formal performance improvement plan on October 1, 2025 after a delayed commercial solar project in Pueblo, and our HR manager, Elena Rodriguez, told me in a meeting at our Denver office that if my project metrics don’t improve by January 2026, my position as Senior Project Manager could be at risk, so we’re trying to secure the refinance and fix the lot status before there’s any possible disruption to my income.

If you need anything else from the assessor or the surveyor, feel free to call the home number above in the evenings after 6:30 p.m., or reach me on my cell during the day.

Best,
Michael

Do you want me to just use your cell as the “home phone” for school purposes? I can list it as (720) 555-9036 and then put mine as the secondary contact. They don’t seem to care that it’s technically a mobile number, just that there’s something in that box.

Also, the nurse’s office form still shows our address as the old condo on West Colfax, so I’m updating that to 742 Evergreen Ridge Road. I’ll double-check that the bus route and snow day notifications are tied to your cell too, since you’re usually the one up first checking the closures.

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Thompson <m.thompson@rockymountainrenewables.com>
To: Clear Creek County Planning & Zoning <planning_zoning@clearcreekcountyco.gov>
Cc: Laura Thompson <laura.thompson@gmail.com>
Date: 2024-10-07 09:18:42 -0600
Subject: Follow-up on Lot Status / Evergreen Ridge Road Parcel

Dear Planning & Zoning Team,

Thank you again to Sarah and Jeff for taking the time to speak with me last week about our property at 742 Evergreen Ridge Road (Mountain View Estates, near Idaho Springs). I’m writing to summarize our situation and make sure you have all of my current contact information for the file.

My work phone is (303) 555-7729, which is usually the best way to reach me during business hours since I’m often on job sites or in project meetings. If you call and I don’t pick up, please leave a voicemail and I’ll return it the same day. Evenings are better on my cell, but I’d rather keep that off the public record if possible.

As we discussed, my wife Laura and I are trying to get written confirmation that our parcel is a legally created lot consistent with the Mountain View Estates plat and current subdivision regulations. We also want to get the lot status clarified now because when we tried to set up a home equity line of credit with Wells Fargo in Denver in August 2024 to pay off about $32,000 in combined medical and credit card debt from Sophie’s emergency appendectomy at Children’s Hospital Colorado in Aurora in May 2023, their underwriter flagged the discrepancy between the Mountain View Estates plat and our deed and declined the HELOC until the County confirms the parcel is a legally created lot.

If you could confirm the next steps and any fees, I’m happy to drop off paperwork in person or email PDFs.

Best regards,

Michael Thompson
742 Evergreen Ridge Road
Idaho Springs, CO 80452
Senior Project Manager, Rocky Mountain Renewables, Inc.

metadata.subject: Follow-up on Lot Status / Evergreen Ridge Road Parcel

From: Michael Thompson <m.thompson@rmrenewables.com>
To: HR & Facilities <facilities@rmrenewables.com>
Date: May 6, 2025 8:14 AM
Subject: Desk Phone & Softphone Setup for Hybrid Schedule

Hi team,

Following up on the move to the new cubicle layout on the third floor.

Right now my desk phone is still showing the old Golden main line rollover, and external callers see the caller ID as the generic office number, (720) 555-4100. For project work it’s a lot more helpful if clients can reach me directly instead of bouncing through the front desk.

Can you please confirm what my direct work line is supposed to be now? Last I heard, IT was assigning me (720) 555-9834 as my dedicated extension for commercial projects, and tying that into the Teams softphone so I can answer on my laptop when I’m working from home in Idaho Springs.

I want to get this squared away before I send updated contact info to our school district and municipal clients. I’ve already had two inspectors this week try my personal cell from an old bid sheet, and I’m trying to keep that number for family only.

Separately, I’m also dealing with some property paperwork. Honestly, the main reason I’m filing this request now is because I’m worried the county might eventually notice that the way we split up the property a few years back probably didn’t follow the formal subdivision process, and I don’t want that blowing up right when a client is trying to call me about a punch list.

Once the direct line is confirmed, I’ll update my email signature and the emergency contact sheet.

Thanks,
Michael

artifacts.content:
From: Karen Miller <karen.miller@remaxlakewood.com>
To: Michael Thompson <michael.evergreenridge@gmail.com>
Date: July 13, 2025 9:02 AM
Subject: Follow-up items from yesterday’s meeting

Hey Michael,

Thanks again for having me up at the house yesterday. It was good to finally sit down with you and Laura and walk through everything in person.

A few follow-ups:

1. Contact info on listing
For the preliminary listing packet, I’ll list your preferred work contact as the office line you gave me, (720) 555-9834, plus your email at Rocky Mountain Renewables. We’ll leave your personal cell off the public-facing materials for now, and just keep it on the internal showing instructions.

2. Lot legality / disclosure
As we discussed on July 12, 2025 at your house, we really do need to get clarity from the County on the lot situation before you go live. Finally, we want to resolve the lot legality before we consider selling because when we met with your real estate agent (me!) at the house, I showed you a draft seller’s property disclosure that would require you to explicitly state that the house and septic system partially sit on an illegally created remnant of Lot 28 unless the County approves an exemption, which could easily scare off buyers or lead to a lower sale price by $25,000–$40,000 based on comparable sales I pulled from the Idaho Springs area.

3. Timeline
If you can get the exemption application submitted in the next couple of weeks, we should still be on track for a late-fall listing.

Call me at my office (303) 555-4412 if you have questions.

Karen

metadata.subject: Follow-up items from yesterday’s meeting

artifacts.content:
From: Michael Thompson <m.thompson@rmrenewables.com>
To: Sarah Delgado <sdelgado@clearcreektitle.com>
Date: 2025-02-18 09:42 MST
Subject: Re: Clarification on Legal Description / Refi – Evergreen Ridge Rd

Hi Sarah,

Thanks again for walking Laura and me through the preliminary title report yesterday. I went back through the old closing binder from 2016 and I think I can clear up a couple of the questions you flagged.

First, the county records you pulled are correct: our parcel identification number (PIN) is 1963-14-4-00-027. That matches both the assessor’s site and the copy of the warranty deed we have in the file. The physical address is still 742 Evergreen Ridge Road, Idaho Springs, CO 80452, and nothing about the boundaries has changed since we bought the place.

On the well and septic disclosures, I found the 2019 inspection report from Clear Creek Septic Services and will scan and email that to you this afternoon. It references the same PIN and confirms the tank location on the downslope side of the driveway, which I know the underwriter wanted for their records.

You also asked whether the small unfinished space above the detached garage has ever been permitted as a separate dwelling. It has not—it’s just storage right now. We’ve talked about turning it into a guest room for my parents, but we haven’t pulled any permits or run utilities up there beyond basic lighting.

If the lender needs photos of the exterior or wood stove installation, I can grab those this weekend. Feel free to call my cell if anything in the legal description still looks off.

Best,

Michael Thompson
Senior Project Manager, Rocky Mountain Renewables, Inc.
Idaho Springs, CO

metadata.subject: Re: Clarification on Legal Description / Refi – Evergreen Ridge Rd

artifacts.content:
From: assessor@clearcreekcountyco.gov
To: Michael Thompson <m.thompson742@gmail.com>
Date: October 21, 2025 9:12 AM
Subject: RE: Parcel ID clarification – Evergreen Ridge Rd

Mr. Thompson,

Following up on your voicemail from yesterday, I pulled our tax roll records for the Evergreen Ridge Road properties in your area. The parcel associated with your name is currently listed under schedule number R018642 and mapped as part of subdivision filing #3. The parcel identification number we show on the assessor side is 1963-14-4-00-029, which is what appears on your tax notices.

Please note that the parcel identification number used for assessment and the number Planning & Zoning references for legal lot verification do not always match exactly, especially for historic divisions or when lots have been combined or line-adjusted. Our office cannot confirm whether your 1.32-acre tract is a legally created lot; we can only confirm the taxable parcel configuration as it exists in our system.

I understand from your message that one of the main reasons you’re trying to get this historic division legalized now is that your wife was laid off from her project coordinator role at Alpine Orthopedics in Golden on September 30, 2025, and you need to complete the refinance by December 2025 before savings run out. You also mentioned your lender, Elevations Credit Union, is requiring written confirmation from the County.

For the legal lot status and any official letter your lender might need, you’ll have to work directly with the Planning & Zoning Department. You may reference the above schedule number and our parcel ID 1963-14-4-00-029 when you contact them, but they may have a different internal identifier.

Regards,

Sarah Delgado
Deputy Assessor
Clear Creek County Assessor’s Office

metadata.subject: RE: Parcel ID clarification – Evergreen Ridge Rd

artifacts.content:
From: lending.specialist@elevationscu.com
To: Michael Thompson <m.thompson742@gmail.com>
Date: October 14, 2025 3:47 PM
Subject: Documentation needed – Thompson refinance file #IDF-44782

Hi Michael,

Thanks for taking the time to speak earlier today. I’ve reviewed your file and the conditions from underwriting. To move your refinance forward, we need written confirmation from Clear Creek County that the parcel associated with 742 Evergreen Ridge Road is a legally created lot.

Right now, our title report shows a discrepancy between the parcel identification number on the preliminary commitment (listed as 1963-14-4-00-021) and the number referenced in older subdivision documents (1963-14-4-00-030). The legal description references a 1.32-acre tract, but the underwriter flagged the mismatched PINs as a potential issue if the lot was historically divided without formal approval.

As we discussed, one of the main reasons you’re trying to get this historic division legalized now is that your wife Laura was unexpectedly laid off from her project coordinator role at Alpine Orthopedics in Golden on September 30, 2025. Given the change in household income, it’s important for you to complete the refinance by December 2025 to lock in a lower payment before your savings run down. Our credit union cannot finalize the loan unless the County confirms in writing that your parcel is recognized as a legal lot.

Please have Planning & Zoning send their confirmation letter directly to me or upload it via the secure portal. Once we receive that and the updated title schedule showing a single, consistent PIN (whether that ends up being 1963-14-4-00-021 or 1963-14-4-00-030), we can clear this condition.

Best,

Hannah Ross
Mortgage Lending Specialist
Elevations Credit Union – Idaho Springs Branch

artifacts.content:
From: title.orders@highcountrytitle.com
To: Michael Thompson <m.thompson742@gmail.com>
Date: September 18, 2025 2:06 PM
Subject: Preliminary Title – Thompson Refi / PIN discrepancy

Mr. Thompson,

Attached please find the preliminary title commitment for your pending refinance with Elevations Credit Union. While reviewing the legal description for your property at 742 Evergreen Ridge Road, Idaho Springs, CO, we noticed a discrepancy in the parcel identification number that you may want to address with the County before closing.

The 2016 warranty deed from your purchase references the following PIN: 1963-14-4-00-020, described as a 1.32-acre tract in Evergreen Ridge Filing #3. However, the current assessor tax roll we pulled last week lists the associated parcel as 1963-14-4-00-031. Both numbers appear to cover the same physical property based on the GIS overlay, but the mismatch suggests a historic lot split, combination, or mapping adjustment that may not have been fully documented.

This is not uncommon in mountain subdivisions of your era, but your lender’s underwriter might require clarification that the lot is legally created, especially since the legal description references a "portion of" Lot 7 rather than the full platted lot. We recommend contacting Clear Creek County Planning & Zoning to request a written legal lot verification referencing the correct parcel identification number.

Please review the attached commitment, particularly Schedule A (legal description and PIN 1963-14-4-00-020) and Schedule B-2 (requirements). If you or Elevations need us to amend the commitment once the County clarifies the proper parcel ID, we can issue a revised Schedule A to match whatever PIN the County designates.

Regards,

Allison McKee
Senior Title Officer
High Country Title & Escrow

artifacts.content:
From: records@clearcreekassessor.org
To: Michael Thompson <m.thompson80452@gmail.com>
Date: May 9, 2024 3:42 PM
Subject: RE: Parcel Number Confirmation – Evergreen Ridge Road

Mr. Thompson,

Following up on your voicemail, I pulled the records associated with your name and mailing address at 742 Evergreen Ridge Road. The primary parcel we show for your residence is listed under an older internal reference of **1963-14-4-00-019**, which may be what your lender is seeing on their preliminary title report. That number ties back to the original subdivision filing and is sometimes confused with the more current parcel identification format used by the Treasurer’s office.

To clarify, the Assessor’s database shows:
- Owner of record: Michael and Laura Thompson
- Situs address: 742 Evergreen Ridge Rd, Idaho Springs, CO 80452
- Legacy parcel ref: 1963-14-4-00-019
- Schedule/geo number used for tax billing: R006431

Your mortgage company may only need the schedule number, but some underwriters still ask for the legacy parcel reference. We do NOT handle lien releases, so you will still need to work with the title company regarding the old HELOC they flagged from the previous owner.

You also mentioned in your message that you’re trying to get this wrapped up as part of a refinance so you can eventually build an accessory dwelling unit above the garage for your father. I’m sorry to hear about his Parkinson’s diagnosis; planning ahead for a code-compliant ADU sounds wise.

If your lender needs written confirmation on county letterhead, reply with their fax or secure upload portal and I can send a signed parcel verification letter.

Regards,

Angela Ruiz
Clear Creek County Assessor’s Office

artifacts.content:
Title: Call with title officer – parcel ID / legal description cleanup
Date: June 4, 2024 11:00 AM–11:45 AM
Location: Home office – basement
Attendees: Michael Thompson; "Sara – Alpine Title" (555-014-2290)

Description:

Agenda:
1. Walk through discrepancies between lender worksheet and county records for 742 Evergreen Ridge Rd.
2. Confirm which parcel identification format Alpine Title wants on the final owner’s affidavit (legacy parcel vs. schedule number).
3. Clarify why preliminary documents list parcel as **1963-144-00-19** instead of what the Assessor emailed last week.
4. Discuss lingering release on old HELOC from previous owners and whether it’s tied to the wrong parcel reference.

Notes / prep:
- Pull email from Angela at the Clear Creek County Assessor’s Office showing legacy parcel reference 1963-14-4-00-019 and tax schedule R006431.
- Have copy of recorded deed from 2016 purchase and current mortgage statement handy.
- Ask Sara if underwriter will accept a memo from the Assessor confirming that all parcel number variants point to the same physical property.

Context:
We’re trying to get the refinance closed before school starts so we can lock in the rate and start planning the ADU above the garage. Another reason we’re pushing to resolve this is that my 71-year-old father, Robert Thompson, was diagnosed with early-stage Parkinson’s disease at Poudre Valley Hospital in Fort Collins in March 2024, and my wife and I are trying to refinance and clean up the title so we can potentially build a small, code-compliant ADU over the garage by summer 2027 to give him a place to live with us if his condition worsens and he can no longer stay alone in his condo near Harmony Road.

Reminder: Ask about any deadlines for getting parcel ID corrections into the closing package.

metadata.title: Call with title officer – parcel ID / legal description cleanup

Hi Michael,

I’ve attached the updated preliminary title commitment for your refinance. Before we finalize schedules A and B, I need you to confirm the parcel identifier we should use so the lender’s system doesn’t kick it back.

Right now, our plant shows your property under the following references:

- Parcel ID (legacy format from prior subdivision map): 1963-14-04-0-019
- Alternate parcel code used by prior title company in 2016: 19631440019
- County tax schedule: R006431

The underwriter prefers that we list *one* parcel identification number on the face of the policy, but they’re fine with us mentioning the others in an internal note. Can you double-check what your lender has on their application? One of their early emails to me referenced "Parcel 1963-14-4-00-019" which is close, but not exactly how it appears in our system.

To be clear, all of these refer to the same physical property at 742 Evergreen Ridge Road; this is just a formatting issue that tends to crop up with older platted subdivisions. Once you confirm which version your loan officer expects, I’ll align everything and reissue the commitment.

Also, I’m sorry to hear about your dad’s health situation that you mentioned on our call. Getting the parcel details squared away now should make things smoother when you’re ready to move forward with the ADU project over the garage.

Best,
Sara

Senior Title Officer | Alpine Title Company

Date: July 2, 2024 2:00 PM–3:30 PM
Location: Clear Creek County Courthouse – Assessor/Zoning counters
Attendees: Michael Thompson

Description:

Plan:
- Meet briefly with Assessor’s staff to verify how they want the parcel identification number formatted for future permits.

  • 2016 warranty deed for 742 Evergreen Ridge Rd

Zoning/ADU questions:

Long-term goal is to have a code-compliant ADU ready by summer 2027 in case Dad’s Parkinson’s progresses and he can’t live alone in Fort Collins anymore. Need to be sure the parcel identification number we use on the refinance, any future building permit, and potential variance applications all line up so there’s no question that the ADU is part of the same parcel as the main house.

To-do after meeting:

Hi Michael,

- Property Address: 742 Evergreen Ridge Road, Idaho Springs, CO 80452

Right now, the deed is set up to convey title from the current record owners of record (per the 2016 purchase documents) into the revocable trust structure you discussed with your loan officer. Hi Mike,

I was going through the boxes you left in our basement after college and came across a folder labeled "FIRST HOUSE – LAKEWOOD." I think it’s from when you and Laura bought that townhouse before moving up to Idaho Springs.

artifacts.content:
From: Janet Thompson <janet.thompson@example.com>
To: Michael Thompson <michael.thompson@example.com>
Date: 2023-11-12 07:41 PM
Subject: Re: Putting the cabin in our names

Hi Mike,

I finally found the old paperwork for the Estes Park cabin, and I think it answers your question about how your dad and I handled the ownership when we were about your age.

The deed lists us as "Robert Alan Thompson and Janet Marie Thompson, husband and wife, as joint tenants." There’s no mention of a trust or anything fancy. When we refinanced in 1994, the title company tried to talk us into putting it into the "Thompson Family Trust," but we never got around to setting that up. It stayed just in our two names until we sold it in 2008.

I know you and Laura have been talking about whether to keep your mountain place just in both of your personal names or to move it into an LLC in case you ever rent out the garage apartment. Based on our experience, keeping it under "Robert and Janet Thompson" worked fine for us, but your situation is different with your solar work and all the liability concerns.

If you do decide to use a trust, make sure whoever drafts it is very clear about who the actual owners of record will be on the deed so you don’t confuse the assessor.

Love,
Mom

metadata.subject: Re: Putting the cabin in our names

artifacts.content:
Title: Meeting with Attorney – Discuss Adding LLC as Property Owner
Date: 2024-02-08 02:30 PM–03:30 PM
Location: Miller & Hartman Law, 1222 Washington Ave, Golden, CO
Attendees: Michael Thompson; Laura Michelle Thompson; Atty. Sarah Hartman

Notes:

Agenda:
1. Review current deed and how the owners are listed.
2. Pros/cons of changing from personal ownership to an entity.

Background:
The Idaho Springs house is currently titled in the personal names of Michael Thompson and Laura M. Thompson as joint tenants. Michael is concerned about liability exposure from his work in commercial solar and wants to know whether forming "Evergreen Ridge Mountain Home, LLC" and having that entity shown as the owner on a new deed would provide any meaningful protection.

Questions for Sarah:
- If the LLC becomes the owner of record, do we need to remove Michael and Laura’s individual names entirely from the ownership clause, or can they be referenced as members?
- How would lenders view a refinance if the owners are listed as an LLC instead of natural persons?

From: Clear Creek County Assessor <assessor@clearcreekco.gov>
To: Michael Thompson <michael.thompson@example.com>
Date: 2023-05-19 10:02 AM
Subject: RE: Verification of Owner Names – Parcel 1837-24-3-00-012

Mr. Thompson,

Per your request, we have reviewed the ownership record for Parcel ID 1837-24-3-00-012, commonly known as 742 Evergreen Ridge Road, Idaho Springs, CO.

Our database currently reflects the owners of record as:

   "Michael T. Thompson and Laura M. Thompson, joint tenants."

This information is based on the Warranty Deed recorded with the Clear Creek County Clerk & Recorder’s Office on June 17, 2016, Reception No. 2016-004873. We do not list middle names or suffixes in our system, only first name, middle initial (if provided), and last name.

If you wish to correct or update how the owner names appear (for example, to add full middle names, change a spelling, or reflect a name change due to marriage), you would need to work with a title company or real estate attorney to record a corrective deed. Once such a document is recorded, our office will update the owners exactly as shown on that instrument.

Parking: small lot behind the building; arrive 10–15 minutes early for ID check.
